\u2014 the EVM lane's single-leg, custody-less path into rome-dex.","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"DEX_PROGRAM()":{"notice":"The one Solana program this router will ever invoke."},"_deposit(address,bytes32,uint64,uint64,uint64)":{"notice":"external-for-self so zapIn can reuse the deposit meta assembly with the original user's ATAs. Reverts for any other caller."},"pendingOwner()":{"notice":"Two-step ownership: proposed next owner, until it accepts. Lets the owner hand control to a multisig/timelock without risking a typo-locked address."},"registerPool(bytes32,bytes32[8])":{"notice":"Add-only: a live pool's accounts can never be silently overwritten. To correct a mis-registration, freeze() is the lock; there is no in-place edit."},"route(bytes32,bool,bytes32,bool,uint64,uint64)":{"notice":"Atomic 2-pool route (e.g. USDC\u2192SOL on one tier, SOL\u2192USDC on another \u2014 or A\u2192B\u2192C across pairs). The mid amount is read back on-chain, so the second hop swaps exactly what the first yielded. One EVM tx, atomic."},"swapExactOut(bytes32,bool,uint64,uint64)":{"notice":"Exact-out (on-chain tag 6): deliver exactly `amountOut`, spend \u2264 `maxIn`."},"zapIn(bytes32,bool,uint64,uint64,uint64)":{"notice":"Atomic zap-in: swap `amountIn` of the input side, then deposit BOTH sides for LP \u2014 one EVM tx, all-or-nothing. The realized swap output AND the pool state are read back on-chain (account_u64_at), so the LP amount is computed from post-swap reserves \u2014 no stale off-chain quote can strand the deposit. `minLp` is the slippage floor; `maxOther` bounds the pre-held other-side spend."}},"notice":"Raw `CPI.invoke` calldata carries ~96B per account meta \u2192 a 14-account swap         is 1540B, larger than a whole Solana tx (1232B), so the proxy must holder-         stage it into 4 legs. This router stores each pool's fixed accounts once and         assembles the metas in EVM memory \u2014 user calldata drops to ~130B and the tx         fits a single atomic leg.         Security model (proven on-chain, harness/probe-delegate.mjs):         \u2022 custody-less \u2014 tokens move user-ATA \u2192 user-ATA; the router holds nothing.         \u2022 the user grants the router's external_auth PDA an SPL delegate allowance           (ERC20-approve-style); the router CPIs with that PDA as the transfer           authority and Rome auto-signs it (a contract can sign only its OWN PDA \u2014           the send-gate rejects origin-PDA signing, so calling a contract grants it           nothing without an explicit approve).         \u2022 every user-side ATA is derived from msg.sender on-chain \u2014 no account           injection: a victim's allowance cannot be routed to an attacker.         \u2022 one hardcoded swap program, fixed instruction shapes \u2014 this is NOT an           arbitrary-invoke passthrough (that is the raw precompile's job).","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/RomeDexRouter.sol":"RomeDexRouter"},"evmVersion":"cancun","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"src/RomeDexRouter.sol":{"keccak256":"0x324c1cee69f908437ddebe15a390adc71557e7f90ddda0afcbea51dd56fa7f88","license":"Apache-2.0","urls":["bzz-raw://3b4401234ef94a1541d286da44cea5c994e59af085c0199dc6dd04f2aeca2a6d","dweb:/ipfs/QmUDTiP1ns6nxNE7dYsTGhNzLvmBUpqCTgTV3Cw34u7WE1"]}},"version":1}

DEX_PROGRAM().notice: The one Solana program this router will ever invoke.

_deposit(address,bytes32,uint64,uint64,u.notice: external-for-self so zapIn can reuse the deposit meta assembly with the original user's ATAs. Reverts for any other caller.

pendingOwner().notice: Two-step ownership: proposed next owner, until it accepts. Lets the owner hand control to a multisig/timelock without risking a typo-locked address.

registerPool(bytes32,bytes32[8]).notice: Add-only: a live pool's accounts can never be silently overwritten. To correct a mis-registration, freeze() is the lock; there is no in-place edit.

route(bytes32,bool,bytes32,bool,uint64,u.notice: Atomic 2-pool route (e.g. USDC→SOL on one tier, SOL→USDC on another — or A→B→C across pairs). The mid amount is read back on-chain, so the second hop swaps exactly what the first yielded. One EVM tx, atomic.

zapIn(bytes32,bool,uint64,uint64,uint64).notice: Atomic zap-in: swap `amountIn` of the input side, then deposit BOTH sides for LP — one EVM tx, all-or-nothing. The realized swap output AND the pool state are read back on-chain (account_u64_at), so the LP amount is computed from post-swap reserves — no stale off-chain quote can strand the deposit. `minLp` is the slippage floor; `maxOther` bounds the pre-held other-side spend.
